content.claim: Cross-national empirical studies find early tracking is associated with increased inequality of opportunity; meta-analyses show tracking widens social achievement gaps (6.90 score points, effect size d=0.44 between students from families with few versus many books).
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Effect on mean performance levels remains contested across replications
Cross-national correlational evidence cannot establish causation within Hungarian context
Meta-analyses aggregate heterogeneous country contexts with different institutional configurations
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
